disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A single larger oval scaly patch appeared on the left flank about 10 days ago (initially mistaken for ringworm), followed 24-48 hours ago by a sudden widespread eruption of many smaller oval papules and plaques across the trunk.
trigger_question_examples: When did the rash start, and did it all come up at once or did one spot appear first?
Tell me the story of how the rash came on over time.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started with one bigger scaly oval patch on my left side about 10 days ago that I thought was ringworm, and then in the last day or two hundreds of smaller spots suddenly erupted across my chest, back and tummy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lesions are small (1-2 cm) oval salmon-coloured papules and plaques, each with a fine trailing collarette of scale just inside the lesion border, and the long axes of the oval lesions follow the skin cleavage lines on the back.
trigger_question_examples: What do the individual spots look like — colour, shape, and any scale?
Is there a pattern to how the spots are lined up on your back?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're small oval salmon-coloured spots with a fine rim of scale just inside the edge, and on my back they seem to line up along the natural creases of my skin.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Moderate pruritus that is worse with sweating and heat; the patient is otherwise systemically well but has severe health anxiety about contagion and a possible severe allergic reaction.
trigger_question_examples: How itchy is it, and does anything make the itch better or worse?
How are you feeling in yourself about all this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's moderately itchy and worse when I get hot or sweaty. I feel okay in myself but I'm really frightened it's a serious allergy or something I could give to other people.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The face, palms and soles are completely spared, there are no mouth or genital sores/patches, and no swollen glands — arguing against secondary syphilis (which classically involves the palms/soles with mucosal patches and lymphadenopathy).
trigger_question_examples: Are there any spots on your palms or the soles of your feet, or any sores in your mouth or on your genitals?
Have you noticed any swollen glands in your neck, armpits or groin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — nothing on my palms or soles, my face is clear, and I've got no sores in my mouth or down below. I haven't noticed any swollen glands.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no feeling systemically unwell, and no blistering, skin peeling or skin pain — arguing against a systemic illness or a severe cutaneous adverse drug reaction (SJS/TEN).
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a fever or feel generally unwell?
Are any of the spots blistering, is the skin peeling, or is any of the rash painful rather than itchy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever and I don't feel unwell in myself. Nothing is blistering or peeling, and it's itchy rather than painful.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new prescription or over-the-counter medications, antibiotics or supplements started in the preceding weeks — arguing strongly against a drug eruption / severe cutaneous adverse drug reaction.
trigger_question_examples: Have you started any new medicines, antibiotics, or supplements in the last few weeks?
Any new tablets or creams around the time the rash started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't started anything new — no new tablets, antibiotics or supplements, and I'm not on any regular medicines.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A mild viral prodrome (fatigue and mild sore throat) about 10 days before the eruption, consistent with a benign post-viral papulosquamous eruption.
trigger_question_examples: Were you unwell at all in the week or two before the rash — a cold, sore throat or feeling run down?
Any viral-type illness before this started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — about 10 days ago I felt run down with a mild sore throat, like the start of a cold, and the first patch came up around then.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The scale is fine and trailing (inward-facing), not thick, adherent or silvery, and there was no preceding streptococcal sore-throat-with-tonsillitis picture — arguing against guttate psoriasis and against widespread tinea (whose scale is outward-facing on the leading edge).
trigger_question_examples: Is the scale thick and silvery, or fine and thin?
Does the scale sit on the inside edge of each spot or on the outer leading edge?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The scale is really fine and thin, not thick or silvery, and it sits just inside the edge of each spot rather than on the outer rim.
